KEY EMPIRICAL FINDING â€” the actual diff mechanism documented across sessions 2-7 was INCOMPLETE / partially mis-framed. Index-aligned objdump diff (`tmp/ab44/canon.ops` vs `tmp/ab44/sand.ops`) shows the cheat-free sandbox build differs from canonical build NOT by $v0/$v1 register choice at case 5/6, but by an entirely different structural pattern: **the sandbox build uses `$s0` as a function-wide return-value accumulator that target avoids completely**.

Concrete pattern in sandbox (cheat-free, candidate.c applied):
  - Prologue: `sw $s0, 16(sp); sw $ra, 20(sp); ... move $s0, $zero` (saves $s0, inits to 0)
  - Every case-end j-delay slot: `move $v0, $s0` (instead of target's `move $v0, $zero`)
  - Fail path: `li $s0, -1` (instead of target's `li $v0, -1`)
  - Case 7: `li $s0, 1` (instead of target's `li $v0, 1`)
  - Shared epilogue: `move $v0, $s0; lw $ra, 20(sp); lw $s0, 16(sp); addiu $sp; jr $ra` (instead of target's `lw $ra, 16(sp); addiu $sp; jr $ra` â€” no $s0 frame slot at all)

Target's compilation never materializes $s0 as the return accumulator: each case's `return X;` emits `move $v0, IMMEDIATE` directly in the j-delay slot, and the function frame saves only $ra (16-byte slot, not 20). Sandbox's frame is 24 bytes either way (sp adjustment), but layout differs (target: ra@16; sandbox: s0@16, ra@20).

This means session 2's diff cluster pinning (the 7-slot diff at offsets 0xb8-0xc4 + 0x130/0x138/0x13c with `$v0`/`$v1` swaps) was reading a SECONDARY artifact â€” the case 5/6 increment register and case 2 polarity diffs are CONSEQUENCES of the $s0 accumulator allocation cascading through the function's RA. The regfix rules ($2<->$3 swap @ 72-75 and the case 2 polarity substs) patch the secondary artifacts; the underlying mechanism that produces ALL of them is the $s0 accumulator's existence.

MECHANISM (re-framed): cc1's `expand_return` allocates one pseudo for the function's return value. With 8 distinct `return X;` statements (6Ã—0, 1Ã—1, 1Ã—-1, plus implicit trailing `return 0;`), this pseudo has 8 defs spread across the entire function body. global.c's RA computes its livelen as spanning the function and assigns it a callee-save register ($s0). Target's compilation made the OPPOSITE decision â€” the per-case constants land directly in $v0 via `(set v0 const) (return)` patterns that survive cross_jump merge into the shared epilogue. WHY cc1 makes the suboptimal $s0 decision in our compilation but not target's is the open question.
